old_claim.note: Chain Mail Armor provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The Qin evidence is an arsenal-scale bronze-weapon assemblage, especially bronze arrowheads and crossbow parts, rather than chain-mail armor.

source_shape.source_locator: The paper focuses on 40,000 bronze arrowheads from the Terracotta Army and discusses bronze weapon manufacture, batches, standardization, and production organization.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source-pinned armory evidence is a large Qin bronze-weapon production and logistics assemblage.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Bronze working is the material capability visible in the source, while chain-mail armor is a separate armor technology not needed for Qin state weapon workshops.

ontology_before: State Armories depended on Chain Mail Armor, laundering a specific flexible-armor technology into a broader state arsenal and weapon-production node.
ontology_after: State Armories is anchored to Qin bronze weapon workshops and arsenal logistics, with Bronze Working as the source-backed material capability.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: state_armories now depends on bronze_working and no longer directly depends on chain_mail_armor.
would_reject_if: The node were later rescoped specifically to chain-mail production or storage.
A stronger source showed the earliest state-armory scope in this graph required chain-mail armor rather than bronze weapon production.
The bronze_working node were narrowed away from bronze weapons and fittings.
short_reason: The source supports Qin bronze weapon production, not chain-mail armor.
